---
title: "Jeffrey Pullin"
about:
  template: trestles
  image: "jeffrey-headshot.jpg"
  links:
    - icon: bluesky
      href: https://bsky.app/profile/jeffreypullin.bsky.social
    - icon: github
      href: https://github.com/jeffreypullin
    - icon: "{{< ai google-scholar >}}"
      href: https://scholar.google.co.uk/citations?user=ruDb6ZsAAAAJ&hl=en
format:
  html:
    page-layout: full
navbar: true
listing:
  contents: post
  sort: "date desc"
  type: grid
  categories: false
  feed: true
  sort-ui: false
  filter-ui: false
  max-items: 3
---

I am currently a PhD student, funded by a [Gates Cambridge Scholarship](https://www.gatescambridge.org/), in the [MRC Biostatistics Unit](https://www.mrc-bsu.cam.ac.uk/) at the University of Cambridge, supervised by [Dr Chris Wallace](https://chr1swallace.gitlab.io/people/). My thesis focuses on improving statistical methodology for mechanistically interpreting GWAS identified loci. Specifically, I am developing the eQTL mapping software [quasar](https://github.com/jeffreypullin/quasar) and contributing to the colocalisation methodology implemented in [coloc](https://github.com/chr1swallace/coloc). I am aiming to finish my PhD in summer 2027.

Previously, I completed a Master of Science (Mathematics and Statistics) at the University of Melbourne. My thesis ([paper](https://link.springer.com/article/10.1186/s13059-024-03183-0)), supervised by Dr Davis McCarthy, compared different statistical methods for selecting genes that distinguish biological cell-types in single-cell RNA-sequencing data. I won the Dwight Prize in Mathematical Statistics as the highest-scoring student in the statistics and stochastic processes specialization in the MSc. I completed my BSc (Mathematics and Statistics) at the University of Melbourne in 2018. 

I have also worked as a:

*  Senior Analyst/Statistician at the Victorian Agency for Health Information, part of the Victorian Department of Health, 
*  Graduate at the Australian Institute of Health and Welfare, and,
*  Research assistant (Part-time) in Wayne Crismani’s Lab at the St Vincent’s Institute of Medical Research.

You can find me [on Bluesky](https://bsky.app/profile/jeffreypullin.bsky.social) or email me at jeffrey.pullin[at]gmail.com

## Education

**PhD in Biostatistics**<br>
University of Cambridge, 2023 ---<br>

**Master of Science (Mathematics and Statistics)**<br>
University of Melbourne, 2021

**Bachelor of Science**<br>
University of Melbourne, 2018
